chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Line item
triplets.object: Unit price at time of order

triplets.subject: Return Policy
triplets.object: products to be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: An order line item is the unit of a Sales Order that references a single Product, representing one item within the order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | **Must contain at least one Order Line Item** | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items

entities_post_resolution.name: Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: An Order is the customer-initiated transaction in an e-commerce system that records a purchase (including the unit price at the time the order is placed) and may be refunded if the order is cancelled.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled. | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID is the self-referencing parent category ID column in the TB_CATEGORY table, used to form a hierarchical category structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
| PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES |

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Payment is a monetary transaction that settles a Sales Order, with one or more payments potentially linked to a single order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
References exactly one Sales Order | Payment amount | Confirmation timestamp | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is a unit of delivery associated with a Sales Order, identified by a tracking number, representing a single delivery event (with a Sales Order potentially having multiple shipments).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
References exactly one Sales Order | Tracking number | Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: Customer Address: the physical or postal address associated with a customer used as the delivery destination for shipments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Definition: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Customer | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | # Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────

entities_post_resolution.name: Full Name
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the customer's full name as stored in the customer data record.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO |
| FULL_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Customer's full name | NO |
| EMAIL | VARCHAR(150) | Unique email address for login | NO |
| REGION_CODE | VARCHAR(10) | Geographic region for tax | YES |
| CREATED_AT | DATETIME | Account creation timestamp | NO |
| IS_ACTIVE | BOOLEAN | Whether customer can place orders | NO |
 | **Key Attributes**:
- Human-readable product name

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price At
entities_post_resolution.definition: Extended amount (line total) on an order line, calculated as the quantity of units ordered multiplied by the unit price at the time of the order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Return Policy: a policy that allows customers to return products within a specified window (e.g., 30 days) in exchange for a full refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: Billing Addresses
entities_post_resolution.definition: A set of physical addresses associated with a customer used for order delivery and billing, allowing multiple addresses with one primary per address type (delivery and billing).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase

entities_post_resolution.name: Active
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status

entities_post_resolution.name: Business Concept Mapping
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

entities_post_resolution.name: Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID)
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID)
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
| TOTAL_AMT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Total order value | NO |
| STATUS_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | PENDING/CONFIRMED/SHIPPED/DELIVERED/CANCELLED | NO |
| PAYMENT_CONFIRMED_AT | DATETIME | Payment confirmation timestamp | YES |
| DELIVERED_AT | DATETIME | Delivery timestamp | YES |
| CREATED_AT | DATETIME | Order creation timestamp | NO |
 | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core information about customers, including their identifiers, contact details, regional assignment, account status, and when the record was created.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, stock-keeping units, names, category association, pricing, and whether the product is active.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores categories and their hierarchical relationships, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores header information for sales orders, including customer reference, order date, total amount, current status, and timestamps for payment confirmation, delivery, and record creation.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores line-by-line details for each order, including which product was ordered, the quantity, unit price, and the calculated line amount.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments processed for customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments associated with orders, including where they are fulfilled, when they were shipped, tracking details, and shipment status.
